title: Tara cacalaco
taxonomy: Tara cacalaco is a member of the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Tara, a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. Tara cacalaco is a species within the Tara genus, which is characterized by its small, white flowers and edible fruit. The plant is often found in tropical forests, where it grows as a small tree or shrub. Tara cacalaco's classification is further defined by its botanical name, Tara cacalaco, which is used to identify the species in a scientific context.

The health benefits of Tara cacalaco include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health due to its rich content of fiber, antioxidants, and flavonoids.
